evals.prompt: My project docs are getting messy — there are markdown files scattered at the root and the docs folder has inconsistent naming. Can you audit the documentation structure and give me a report of everything that needs fixing? Don't change anything yet, just the audit.
evals.expected_output: Structured audit report identifying: root violations (REFACTOR_PLAN.md, UI_ANALYSIS.md) with destinations, lowercase archived/ folder, setup-guide.md naming, tasks_completed/ naming (deferred to pm-tasks), missing docs/README.md. No files modified.
assertions: Identifies REFACTOR_PLAN.md and UI_ANALYSIS.md as root violations and proposes docs/ destinations for each
Flags lowercase docs/archived/ for rename to ARCHIVED/
Flags docs/tasks_completed/ as task-related naming issue WITHOUT reorganizing its content (delegation boundary)
Made zero file modifications (audit is read-only)
Report is structured (tables/sections with summary), not free prose

evals.prompt: Can you check the state of tasks in this project? I think there are TODOs scattered in the code and in random markdown files, and my TODO.md is probably stale. Give me a full report of what you find and what you'd centralize — don't change anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Report finding: 3 code TODOs (one cross-referenced to UI-2), TODO.md at root flagged, README Roadmap contamination, spec.md task checkboxes flagged, setup-guide.md checkboxes deemed legitimate, tasks_completed naming issues + code block, completed CI task ready to archive. No files modified.
assertions: Finds all 3 code TODOs: rate limiting (auth.ts), FIXME offline token refresh (auth.ts), UI-2 resize (chart.tsx)
Cross-references TODO(UI-2) in chart.tsx with task UI-2 in TODO.md as already tracked
Flags TODO.md at root as non-standard (should be docs/TASK_TODO.md)
Flags README.md Roadmap checkboxes as task contamination to extract
Flags docs/features/spec.md checkboxes as tasks while classifying docs/setup-guide.md checkboxes as legitimate guide steps
Flags docs/tasks_completed/ naming (should be TASK_COMPLETED/, file should be 2605.md) and/or the code block inside the archive
Identifies completed 'Setup CI pipeline' task as ready to archive
